name: Pinto Beans and Squash Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover the pinto beans and 1 teaspoon of salt with at least 3 inches (inches) of boiling water in a large pot.
2. Add the salt pork, onion, and garlic. Cover and bake at 250°F until the beans are cooked, about 2 to 2 hours and 15 minutes.
3. Season to taste with salt and pepper if necessary.
4. Keep warm.
5. Cook the bacon with jalapeno and green onions over low heat in a large saute pan until the bacon is crisp.
6. Add the squash and stock. Cover and cook until the squash is just tender, about 30 minutes.
7. Combine the beans and squash gently but well. Do not smash the beans or squash.
8. Spoon the mixture into 6 serving bowls. Sprinkle each with red onion, cilantro, and 2 tablespoons of sour cream.
Each serving contains about: 456 calories; 325 milligrams sodium; 25 milligrams cholesterol; 18 grams fat; 57 grams carbohydrates; 20 grams protein; 5.45 grams fiber.
